title: chrysothamnus bloomeri var. angustatus
intro: Chrysothamnus bloomeri var. angustatus, also known as a narrow-leafed greasewood or narrow-leaved greasewood, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to produce a resinous sap that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Chrysothamnus bloomeri var. angustatus include its potential to reduce swelling and alleviate pain, as well as its use in treating infections and wounds. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, often used topically to apply its medicinal properties directly to the affected area.
